skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: A scientist measured the wingspans of 180 birds from Park P and 270 birds from Park Q. The mean wingspan of the 180 birds from Park P was 60 centimeters and the mean wingspan of the 270 birds from Park Q was 75 centimeters. What was the mean wingspan of all 450 birds the scientist measured?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The mean wingspan of all 450 birds equals the total wingspan divided by 450. The total wingspan of the 180 birds from Park P is 180 × 60 = 10,800 centimeters. The total wingspan of the 270 birds from Park Q is 270 × 75 = 20,250 centimeters. Therefore, the combined total is 10,800 + 20,250 = 31,050 centimeters, and the mean wingspan is 31,050 ÷ 450 = 69 centimeters.

Choices A, B, and D are incorrect and result from arithmetic errors.
